records.ety: [F. dgarnir; pref. d-, des- (L. dis-) + garnir to furnish. See Garnish, and cf. Disgarnish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. degarnished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. degarnishing.]

defs: To strip or deprive of entirely, as of furniture, ornaments, etc.; to disgarnish; as, to degarnish a house, etc.
To deprive of a garrison, or of troops necessary for defense; as, to degarnish a city or fort.
